meta.name: Tamroi (ตามรอย)
meta.team: ปลามึกยักษ์
meta.description: Mobile-first gamified Thailand travel web app. Players travel to real Thai districts, check in at Watchtower landmarks to clear Fog of War, visit Support Nodes (local cafes, OTOP shops, landmarks) to unlock encounters, capture historical figures via quiz challenges, and collect Lore by physically visiting GPS-defined Lore points. The app teaches Thai history through Situated Learning and Experiential Learning.

project_context.documentation_rule:
After completing ANY task or sub-task, BEFORE moving to the next one, update all three of these files to reflect the change:
1. progress.md - Mark the task row as ✅ Done (or 🔄 In Progress) - Move it from "Known Gaps" to "What's Working" once complete - Update "Last updated" date at the top
2. CLAUDE.md - If a new game system was added: add or update its entry under "Key Gameplay Mechanics" - If a new file was created: add it to the File Structure block - If DB schema changed: note the new table/trigger
3. AGENTS.md - Update "Known Gaps" section (remove resolved gaps, add any new ones discovered) - Update "Repository Map" if new files were added - Update "Current Implementation Notes" if window.DB or window.AppCore changed
Failure to update these three files makes the next coding session start with stale context. Do not skip this step even for small changes.

product_vision.concept: Tamroi (ตามรอย — "Trace the Footsteps") is an educational location-based game that transforms Thai history learning from passive classroom study into active real-world exploration. The core idea: historical knowledge delivered AT the place where the event occurred creates stronger long-term memory than textbook reading (Situated Learning theory).

learning_frameworks.framework: Knowledge is delivered at real-world locations where historical events occurred. Players must physically travel to Bangkok districts, provinces, and landmarks. Context-based acquisition replaces passive memorization.

1. Concrete Experience — travel to and check-in at real-world locations (GPS) 2. Reflective Observation — read Lore content tied to that location 3. Abstract Conceptualization — complete location-specific quiz questions 4. Active Experimentation — capture Historical Figure cards, earn Legacy Score

1. Collection and Reward — Figure cards with S/A/C rarity tiers 2. Social Competition — High Chronicler leaderboard (Legacy Score, Map %, Archive) 3. Exploration and Discovery — Fog of War creates curiosity gap driving travel 4. Historical Narrative — figures tied to specific regions build mental maps 5. Lore System — GPS-proximity unlocks deepen knowledge at each location

target_users.primary: Ages 14–25 · Thai high school and university students · mobile-first · interested in history and exploration
target_users.secondary_1: Educators and teachers · field-trip organizers · want gamified real-world history learning
target_users.secondary_2: Tour guides and cultural tourism professionals · enhance tours with historical gamification
target_users.design_target: Ages 20–30 · any person interested in Thai history · cultural tourism participants

full_game_systems.system: - Thailand map initially fully fogged (dark inverted polygon over CartoDB tiles) - Fog lifts per district when user physically checks in via GPS - Polygon Matching: user GPS coords vs GeoJSON district polygon - Tolerance Radius (500m) prevents false check-ins at boundaries - 77 province/district polygons total (GeoJSON, simplified for performance) - Map Discovery % = (cleared districts / 77) × 100 — leaderboard metric - Cleared state persists in Supabase user_districts (fogged = false) - On page reload: re-read user_districts and re-punch all cleared holes

- Watchtower = fixed landmark marker in each district - User travels to real location, taps marker → GPS validates within 500m tolerance - Check-in writes to user_districts, sets fogged = false, triggers fog removal - Check-in sheet shows: district name, fog clearing animation, support node progress, and Encounter button if Support Nodes complete - Watchtower also acts as display hub: shows collected figures for that district in two view modes: All Figures / By Historical Period

- C-Class figures: capturable immediately after check-in + 1-question quiz. No gate. - S/A-Class (Legendary): LOCKED until Support Node requirements met for that district: cafes_visited >= 2 → Local Rumors gathered otops_visited >= 1 → Relic obtained landmarks_visited >= 3 → Historical Knowledge built - Support Nodes = cafes, OTOP shops, local landmarks on the map (tappable markers) - Visiting a Support Node increments the counter in user_districts via DB call - When all counters met: Encounter unlock button appears on check-in sheet - Show progress bars per counter while locked (e.g. Café 1/2, OTOP 0/1, Landmark 2/3)

1. GPS proximity trigger OR Watchtower check-in completes 2. Quiz modal appears — question fetched from quiz_questions table (location-specific) 3. C-Class: 1 question. S/A-Class Master Quiz: 3 questions, all must be correct. 4. Correct answer → INSERT into user_captures 5. Supabase DB trigger auto-updates profiles.legacy_score += figures.legacy_pts 6. Show capture success animation (figure card flips in, floats up) 7. Re-render collection grid — newly captured card gets green ribbon, lock removed 8. Leaderboard row for current user updates via Realtime subscription

- Lore nodes are GPS points with a radius_m field (not district-wide) - On each GPS position update: compute Haversine distance to each lore node - If user enters radius AND lore not yet in user_lore table → fire unlock sequence - Unlock sequence: bottom sheet appears with Thai historical narrative + optional media - Content types: text (default), image (lazy-loaded), audio (custom player), document - After user dismisses sheet: INSERT into user_lore, award lore_pts to legacy_score - Multi-site Lore Chains: 3 lore_nodes share a chain_id Collecting all 3 reveals the full consolidated historical story Chain completion awards +50 bonus pts Journal shows chain progress: e.g. "2/3 parts discovered" - Lore Journal: visible in Collection tab → Journal filter pill Lists all unlocked lore entries with date, district, content preview Chains grouped under a chain header card

- Questions stored in quiz_questions table (not hardcoded) - Each question linked to figure_id and district_id - Format: Multiple Choice, 4 options (A/B/C/D), correct_option field - C-Class: fetch 1 question via DB.Quiz.getForFigure(figureId) - S/A Master Quiz: fetch 3 questions, all must be answered correctly in sequence - Validation: client-side (correct_option is readable — score write is server-side trigger) - Failure: show fail state, allow retry (no penalty in Phase 1)

- Figure capture points: Supabase DB trigger on user_captures INSERT → UPDATE profiles SET legacy_score = legacy_score + figures.legacy_pts - Lore points: client-side call DB.Profiles.addLegacyPoints() after user_lore insert - BTS/MRT bonus: ×2 multiplier applied if user checks in near a station polygon - Leaderboard view (Supabase materialized view) aggregates 3 metrics: Legacy Score (primary), Map Discovery % (secondary), Archive Count (tertiary) - Real-time updates: Supabase Realtime subscription on profiles table → update only the affected row in DOM, re-sort if rank changed → unsubscribe when user leaves leaderboard tab

- Trigger points: check-in confirmed, figure captured, rank changed, new lore unlocked, legacy score updated - Stored in notifications table (user_id, title, message, is_read) - Real-time delivery via Supabase Realtime channel on notifications table - Top-bar red dot badge for unread count - Offcanvas panel shows all notifications with unread indicator dots

product_vision.user_journey: Step 1 Login/Sign-in via Google OAuth or Email/Password (Supabase Auth) Step 2 Onboarding: grant location permission, choose home district (stored in localStorage) Step 3 Home district fog cleared automatically as starting base Step 4 Map tab: explore fog-covered Thailand, find Watchtower markers Step 5 Travel to real district → GPS validates within 500m tolerance → check-in Step 6 Fog lifts for that district, encounter options appear Step 7 C-Class: tap figure node → 1-question quiz → capture → +50 pts S/A-Class: visit Support Nodes (café×2, OTOP×1, landmark×3) → all met → Encounter unlocks → Master Quiz (3 questions) → capture → up to +500 pts Step 8 While exploring: walk near Lore nodes → proximity triggers → historical story unlocks → saved to Lore Journal → lore_pts awarded Step 9 Collection tab: view captured figures, artifacts, Lore Journal entries Step 10 Leaderboard tab: see real-time ranking vs other players (3 metrics)
product_vision.known_limitations_from_doc: - GPS accuracy: HTML5 Geolocation API gives 10–50m typically; tolerance radius required - GeoJSON performance: 77 polygons need data simplification and efficient polygon matching - Content management: all historical text, quiz questions, lore narratives need curation - Urban vs rural GPS accuracy variance — may need larger tolerance in rural areas - Desktop lacks native GPS (smartphone is the primary target device)
product_vision.future_phases_summary: Phase 2 (Mobile App): React Native, ARKit/ARCore figure capture, background GPS, QR scanning at OTOP nodes Phase 3 (Co-op): Raid encounters (3+ players), district leaderboards, artifact trading, clan system Phase 4 (Seasonal): Rotating story arcs, era filter toggle (Sukhothai/Ayutthaya/Rattanakosin/Modern), limited-time figures Phase 5 (Business): Film studio tie-ins, certified brand outposts, TAT government sponsorship, green transport XP

current_session_objective.priority_1: Complete the full Lore system (T11 → T15 + T23 + T24) end-to-end: GPS proximity check → Lore unlock bottom sheet → Lore Journal DB persistence → multi-site Lore chain consolidation → Lore Points write.
current_session_objective.priority_2: Add the missing infrastructure the Word doc specifies but the code lacks: Supabase DB trigger for Legacy Score, GPS tolerance radius for check-ins, Supabase Realtime leaderboard subscription, quiz data table in DB.
current_session_objective.priority_3: Fix fog clearing persistence (T01) — the single most visually broken thing because cleared districts reset on every page reload.
current_session_objective.note: Do NOT start new features until the current priority is fully working and tested. Implement one task at a time. After each task, state what file was changed and what the user should see/test to verify it works.

before_coding.step: Read the file you are about to edit FIRST. Never write code from memory.
Check js/supabase-client.js before adding any DB call — the method may already exist.
Check supabase/schema.sql before adding a new table — the column may already exist.
For any new bottom sheet, copy the exact HTML pattern from the existing sheets in app.html.

lore_system_instructions.step: In js/map.js, add a haversineDistance(lat1, lng1, lat2, lng2) private helper function that returns distance in metres. Formula: standard Haversine. Do NOT use a library. Then define a LORE_NODES array (mock data for now, 3–5 entries) where each entry is: { id, name_th, name_en, lat, lng, radius_m: 100, lore_pts: 30, content_type: 'text', content_th: '...', content_en: '...', chain_id: null, chain_part: null }. chain_id and chain_part are for multi-site lore chains (see L5).

Inside the GPS watchPosition callback in MapModule, after updating the user dot, call a private function checkLoreProximity(userLat, userLng). This function iterates LORE_NODES, computes haversineDistance, and if distance is less than node.radius_m AND the node is not already in the user's unlockedLoreIds set → call unlockLore(node). Keep unlockedLoreIds as a module-level Set, populated from DB on init.

In app.html, add a new bottom sheet with id="lore-sheet" using the EXACT same .bottom-sheet + .bottom-sheet-overlay pattern as the existing sheets. Include: - A lore type badge (pill showing content_type) - A Thai title (h3, Kanit font) - A scrollable narrative text block (max-height: 40vh, overflow-y: auto) - Optional: img tag (hidden by default, shown when content_type === 'image') - Optional: audio element with custom play/pause button (hidden by default, shown when content_type === 'audio') - A "บันทึกลง Journal" (Save to Journal) confirm button — primary style - Lore points badge showing how many points were earned Populate and open this sheet from unlockLore(node) in map.js via AppCore.openLoreSheet(node).

In supabase/schema.sql, add these tables (write as a new patch SQL file at supabase/patch_lore.sql — do NOT edit schema.sql directly): CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS lore_nodes ( id uuid PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(), name_th text, name_en text, lat numeric, lng numeric, radius_m integer DEFAULT 100, lore_pts integer DEFAULT 20, content_type text DEFAULT 'text', content_th text, content_en text, chain_id uuid, chain_part integer, district_id uuid REFERENCES districts(id), is_active boolean DEFAULT true ); CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS user_lore ( user_id uuid REFERENCES auth.users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE, lore_id uuid REFERENCES lore_nodes(id), unlocked_at timestamptz DEFAULT now(), PRIMARY KEY (user_id, lore_id) ); ALTER TABLE user_lore ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY; CREATE POLICY "own lore" ON user_lore FOR ALL USING (user_id = auth.uid()); Then in js/supabase-client.js, add a Lore namespace: Lore: { getAll: async () → fetch all active lore_nodes getUserUnlocked: async (userId) → fetch all user_lore rows for this user unlock: async (userId, loreId) → INSERT INTO user_lore (upsert, ignore conflict) } After saving to DB, call DB.Profiles.addLegacyPoints(userId, node.lore_pts).

Multi-site Lore chain: some Lore nodes share a chain_id. A chain has 3 parts. When the last part of a chain is unlocked (chain_part === 3, or all parts for that chain_id are in user_lore), trigger a special "Chain Complete" sheet that combines all 3 narrative pieces into a single scrollable story. Show a bonus of +50 pts. In the Lore Journal UI (Collection tab), display chained entries grouped together under the chain title with a "Complete / X/3 parts" badge. Implement chain completion check inside unlockLore() after the DB write.

In js/collection.js, add a "Journal" tab pill alongside the existing filter pills (All / S-Class / A-Class / C-Class / Artifacts / Journal). When "Journal" is selected, hide the figure grid and show a lore journal list instead. Each journal entry: lore name (Thai), district name, unlock date, content_type badge, first 80 characters of content_th as preview, tap to expand full narrative inline. Chained entries show under a chain header card with a progress bar (e.g. 2/3 parts). Fetch from DB.Lore.getUserUnlocked(userId) joined with lore_nodes data.

step.task: T20 — GPS Tolerance Radius for check-in

missing_systems_instructions.step: The Word doc specifies a Tolerance Radius to prevent false check-ins at district boundaries. In js/map.js, when the user taps the Watchtower check-in button, validate that haversineDistance(userLat, userLng, districtCenter.lat, districtCenter.lng) is less than CHECKIN_TOLERANCE_M (set to 500 metres for now — adjustable). If the user is outside range, show a toast: "คุณอยู่ไกลเกินไป — เดินทางให้ใกล้กว่านี้" and block the check-in. Do NOT block for debug/localhost — add an isDev() guard.

step.task: T21 — Supabase DB Trigger for Legacy Score

missing_systems_instructions.step: The Word doc specifies real-time leaderboard updates via Supabase Realtime Subscriptions. In js/leaderboard.js, after initial load, subscribe to: supabase.channel('leaderboard-updates') .on('postgres_changes', { event: 'UPDATE', schema: 'public', table: 'profiles' }, payload => LeaderboardModule.onScoreUpdate(payload)) .subscribe() onScoreUpdate() should update only the affected player row in the DOM (find by user_id) rather than re-fetching the entire list. Re-sort and re-render only if rank order changed. Unsubscribe when the leaderboard tab is left (store channel reference, call .unsubscribe() in the tab switch handler in app.js).

step.task: T25 — Quiz data as DB table

missing_systems_instructions.step: Currently quiz questions are hardcoded or nonexistent. The Word doc says quiz content must be location-specific and validated server-side. Add to supabase/patch_lore.sql: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS quiz_questions ( id uuid PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(), figure_id uuid REFERENCES figures(id), district_id uuid REFERENCES districts(id), question_th text NOT NULL, option_a text, option_b text, option_c text, option_d text, correct_option char(1) NOT NULL, difficulty text DEFAULT 'C' ); ALTER TABLE quiz_questions ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY; CREATE POLICY "public read quiz" ON quiz_questions FOR SELECT USING (true); Seed at least 2 questions per mock figure in the patch file. In js/supabase-client.js add: Quiz: { getForFigure: async (figureId) → fetch quiz_questions WHERE figure_id = figureId LIMIT 1 } In the C-Class quiz modal (T04), fetch the question from DB.Quiz.getForFigure() instead of using hardcoded questions. Validate the selected option client-side (correct_option is safe to read — it's not a secret, only the score write matters and that is server-side).

core_loop_fix_instructions.step: In js/map.js inside MapModule.init(), after the map is created and before markers load: const clearedDistricts = await DB.Districts.getUserState(userId); clearedDistricts .filter(row => row.fogged === false) .forEach(row => MapModule.punchFogHole(row.district_id)); punchFogHole() must look up the district polygon by id (from the DISTRICTS array or from DB if loading from DB) and remove that polygon from the fog layer. This ensures fog state survives page reload.

In js/map.js, the node info card bottom sheet has a "เยี่ยมชมสถานที่นี้" (Visit) button. When the user taps it, call DB.Districts.updateNodeVisit(userId, districtId, nodeType) where nodeType is 'cafe', 'otop', or 'landmark'. This increments the correct counter in user_districts. Show a toast confirming the visit. Disable the Visit button for already-visited nodes (check against the current user_districts row on sheet open).

In the Watchtower check-in sheet, after the check-in write succeeds, fetch the user_districts row for this district and evaluate: const canUnlock = row.cafes_visited >= 2 && row.otops_visited >= 1 && row.landmarks_visited >= 3; If canUnlock is true, show an "ปลดล็อค Encounter" button (primary orange) that opens the Legendary encounter flow. If false, show a progress section: three rows (Café X/2, OTOP X/1, Landmark X/3) each with a small progress bar. Style as a locked card with --color-muted text.

after_each_task.rule: State exactly which files were modified.
State what the user should open/tap in the browser to verify the feature works.
If you added a SQL patch file, remind the user to run it in Supabase SQL Editor.
Do not move to the next task until verification steps are listed.

tech_stack.frontend: HTML5, Bootstrap 5.3, Vanilla JS (ES6 modules — NO npm, NO build tools)
tech_stack.deployment: Vercel (static + Node.js build.js for env injection)

constraints.item: No npm install, no webpack, no framework — serve with VS Code Live Server
All JS follows IIFE module pattern: const ModuleName = (() => { ... })()
All Supabase calls go through js/supabase-client.js only — never call supabase directly
Always use utils.escapeHtml() for any user-visible string rendered into DOM
No hardcoded hex colors — use CSS variables from css/variables.css only
Max content width 430px, mobile-first, fixed top bar 56px, fixed footer nav 60px
No comments explaining what code does — only write comments for non-obvious WHY
Never commit js/env.js — credentials live in Vercel env vars only
Bottom sheets always use the .bottom-sheet + .bottom-sheet-overlay pattern from app.html

file_structure.file: Splash / landing page with animated blob and CTA
Email + password + Google OAuth login and register
Location permission request + home district picker
Main 4-tab app shell: Map, Collection, Missions, Leaderboard
Vercel build script — injects SUPABASE_URL and SUPABASE_ANON_KEY
Security headers (CSP, HSTS, X-Frame, Permissions-Policy)
Design tokens — colors, spacing, radii, shadows, transitions
Top bar, bottom nav, tab shell structure
Buttons, cards, inputs, bottom sheets, badges, pills
Leaflet overrides, fog layer, markers, GPS dot, node info card
Keyframes: blobMorph, floatY, locationPulse, fadeIn, slideUp
Reads window.ENV → exports window.APP_CONFIG
Credential template — copy to env.js (gitignored) for local dev
escapeHtml() XSS prevention utility
All DB and Auth abstraction: Auth, Profiles, Districts, Figures, Artifacts, Leaderboard, Notifications, Lore, Quiz
Boot sequence, auth guard, tab navigation, toast notifications, openLoreSheet()
Leaflet map, Fog of War inverted polygon, live GPS dot, watchtower markers, node info card, lore proximity check, haversine distance
Historical figures + artifacts grid, filter pills, search, capture states, Lore Journal tab
Active quest steps display + daily challenge list
Podium + ranked list, 3 metric toggles, period filter, Realtime subscription
Full DB schema + Bangkok district seed data — DO NOT EDIT, add patches instead
Auth trigger fix + RLS INSERT policy
NEW — lore_nodes, user_lore, quiz_questions tables + legacy score trigger

database_schema.table: user_id, username, avatar_url, legacy_score, created_at, updated_at
id, name_th, name_en, province, center_lat, center_lng, polygon_coords, is_active
user_id, district_id, fogged, checked_in_at, cafes_visited, otops_visited, landmarks_visited
id, name_th, name_en, class (S/A/C), legacy_pts, image_emoji, district_id, is_active
database_schema.view: Aggregates legacy_score, map discovery %, archive count per user
database_schema.table: id, name_th, name_en, lat, lng, radius_m, lore_pts, content_type, content_th, content_en, chain_id, chain_part, district_id, is_active
user_id, lore_id, unlocked_at — RLS: own rows only
id, figure_id, district_id, question_th, option_a–d, correct_option, difficulty — RLS: public SELECT
database_schema.trigger: AFTER INSERT on user_captures → UPDATE profiles.legacy_score += figures.legacy_pts

game_mechanics.mechanic: Thailand map starts fully fogged. Fog lifts per district when user GPS-checks in. Cleared state persists in user_districts.fogged = false. Discovery % = (cleared districts / total active districts) * 100. Fog polygon is inverted — one giant polygon covering the world with holes punched per district.

Use haversineDistance() to verify user is within CHECKIN_TOLERANCE_M (500m) of district center. watchPosition() for continuous monitoring. Block check-in if outside tolerance. Bypass tolerance check on localhost/dev to allow desktop testing.

C-Class figures: capturable immediately after check-in quiz. No Support Node required. S/A-Class (Legendary): LOCKED until user visits minimum Support Nodes in that district: - 2 cafes visited (cafes_visited >= 2) - 1 OTOP shop visited (otops_visited >= 1) - 3 landmarks visited (landmarks_visited >= 3) Show progress bars for each counter. When all met: show Encounter unlock button.

1. GPS proximity → node tapped OR Watchtower check-in completes 2. Quiz modal appears (question fetched from quiz_questions table) 3. User selects answer → validated client-side 4. Correct: INSERT into user_captures → DB trigger auto-updates legacy_score 5. Show capture success animation (figure card flips in) 6. Update collection grid (re-render captured figure card) 7. Refresh leaderboard row for current user

Lore nodes are GPS points (NOT district-wide). Each has a radius_m proximity trigger. When user enters radius: unlock Lore → show bottom sheet → save to user_lore → award lore_pts. Lore Points written client-side (no trigger) via DB.Profiles.addLegacyPoints(). Multi-site Lore chains: 3 lore_nodes share a chain_id. Collecting all 3 reveals the full historical story and awards a +50 chain completion bonus. Journal: all unlocked lore visible in Collection tab → Journal filter.

Figure capture points: auto-calculated by Supabase DB trigger on user_captures insert. Lore points: added client-side after user_lore insert. BTS/MRT bonus: ×2 multiplier on check-in points if user is near a station polygon. Leaderboard updates via Supabase Realtime subscription — no full page reload needed.

1. Legacy Score — total points from captures + lore (primary ranking) 2. Map Discovery % — (cleared districts / total) * 100 3. Archive Count — total figures captured + artifacts owned Filters: Weekly / Monthly / All-Time. Updates in real time via Supabase Realtime.

Questions stored in quiz_questions table (location/figure-specific). Format: Multiple Choice (4 options A/B/C/D). correct_option field. C-Class: 1 question required. S/A-Class Master Quiz: 3 questions required (all must be correct). Fetch via DB.Quiz.getForFigure(figureId). Validate client-side (correct_option is not secret). Score write is server-side (trigger), so client-side validation is safe.

completed_features.feature: Authentication — email/password + Google OAuth via Supabase, session management, auth guard
Onboarding — location permission request, home district selection
4-tab app shell with smooth tab transitions and lazy-load per tab
Leaflet map with CartoDB Dark tiles centered on Bangkok
Fog of War — inverted polygon clears per district on Watchtower check-in (UI + DB write)
Live GPS tracking dot + accuracy ring, auto-updates on device movement
Watchtower check-in bottom sheet UI with district preview and unlock requirements list
Map nodes (café / OTOP / landmark) with type-specific icons and node info card bottom sheet
Collection grid — historical figures with class badges (S/A/C), capture ribbons, lock overlays
Artifacts horizontal scroll with rarity tiers (legendary / rare / common)
Collection filter pills (All / S-Class / A-Class / C-Class / Artifacts) + search bar
Figure detail modal — portrait, class badge, Thai/English name, legacy points
Active quest display with 4-step progress chain (mock data)
Leaderboard — podium top-3, ranked list, 3 metric toggles, period filter (mock data)
Notification system — top-bar badge, offcanvas panel, unread state tracking
Full CSS design system — variables, layout, components, map, animations
Supabase schema with RLS + auth trigger
Vercel deployment with env var injection and security headers

group.name: Lore System (FC2 — Lore/Proximity Loop)
group.task: GPS proximity trigger — haversineDistance helper + LORE_NODES array + checkLoreProximity() in watchPosition callback. See ai_instructions L1 + L2.
Lore unlock bottom sheet — title, narrative, optional image/audio, Save button, lore pts badge. See ai_instructions L3.
Lore Journal DB persistence (user_lore table) + Journal filter tab in Collection. See ai_instructions L4 + L6.
Lore Points — client-side write to profiles.legacy_score after user_lore insert. See ai_instructions L4.
Rich lore content types — image (lazy img), audio (custom play/pause), document (scrollable block). See ai_instructions L3.
Multi-site Lore chain — 3-node chain, chain completion detection, combined story sheet, +50 bonus, chain progress in Journal. See ai_instructions L5.

group.name: Missing Systems (from Word doc)
group.task: GPS tolerance radius for check-in — 500m Haversine gate, toast if too far, bypass on localhost. See ai_instructions M1.
Supabase DB trigger for Legacy Score on user_captures insert. Remove client-side score write for captures. See ai_instructions M2.
Supabase Realtime subscription on profiles table — update leaderboard DOM on score change, unsubscribe on tab leave. See ai_instructions M3.
Quiz questions DB table — figure-specific questions, seed 2 per mock figure, DB.Quiz.getForFigure(). See ai_instructions M4.

group.name: Core Loop Fixes (FC1 — District Encounter Loop)
group.task: Fog clearing persistence — re-read user_districts on MapModule.init() and re-punch all cleared holes. See ai_instructions C1.
Support Node visit tracking — Visit button in node info card increments cafes/otops/landmarks counter in DB. See ai_instructions C2.
Support Node completion gate — check counters after check-in, show Encounter button or progress bars. See ai_instructions C3.
C-Class quiz modal — fetch from DB.Quiz.getForFigure(), 4-option MCQ, on correct → DB.Figures.capture() → capture animation.
Legendary encounter flow — Master Quiz (3 questions, all correct required) after Support Node gate passes, then S-Class capture.
Legacy Points client write — only for Lore Points now (figure capture handled by DB trigger T21). Expose DB.Profiles.addLegacyPoints().
Real map discovery % — compute from user_districts count in DB, show in stats bar on init and after each check-in.
Collection refresh after capture — re-render only the affected figure card (green ribbon, remove lock).
Leaderboard refresh after score update — handled by Realtime subscription (T22), fallback to LeaderboardModule.load().
BTS/MRT bonus ×2 — station polygons in map.js, check GPS on check-in, apply multiplier before lore_pts write.

group.name: Infrastructure
group.task: Full Thailand district coverage — load polygon_coords from DB instead of hardcode, seed remaining Bangkok districts.
Email verification — re-enable Confirm email in Supabase before NSC demo, test full signup→verify flow.
Vercel production smoke test — full flow with real env vars.
Real-time notifications — Supabase Realtime channel on notifications table, live badge update.

week.step: T11 — haversine helper + LORE_NODES + checkLoreProximity()
T12 — Lore unlock bottom sheet UI
T21 — patch_lore.sql: lore_nodes + user_lore + quiz_questions + legacy_score trigger
T13 — user_lore DB write + Journal tab in Collection
T14 — Lore Points client write
T01 — Fog clearing persistence on reload
T20 — GPS tolerance radius for check-in
T02 — Support Node visit tracking (Visit button → DB)
T03 — Support Node gate (progress bars → Encounter unlock)
T25 — Quiz questions seeding + DB.Quiz.getForFigure()
T04 — C-Class quiz modal end-to-end
T06 — DB.Profiles.addLegacyPoints() for lore (trigger handles captures)
T07 — Map discovery % from DB
T05 — Legendary encounter + Master Quiz (3 questions)
T08 — Collection refresh after capture
T22 — Supabase Realtime leaderboard subscription
T09 — Leaderboard refresh (via Realtime)

T15 — Rich lore content types (image, audio)
